description_long: This object is a compact computer memory module, specifically a SODIMM (Small Outline Dual In-line Memory Module) designed for use in laptops and small form factor computers. The front side features a central integrated circuit (IC) chip, surrounded by smaller surface-mounted components and a green printed circuit board (PCB) with visible traces and gold contact fingers along the bottom edge for insertion into a memory slot. The branding 'Intel' and 'Celeron' are printed on the PCB, indicating it is a memory module compatible with Intel Celeron systems. The back side of the module is mostly flat, with a grid of solder points and additional branding, but lacks the prominent IC chip seen on the front. The module is thin and rectangular, with four mounting holes and a notch in the contact edge for correct orientation during installation. The overall construction is typical of modern electronic memory modules, combining plastic, metal, and silicon materials.
description: A green Intel Celeron SODIMM memory module with a central IC chip, gold contacts, and surface-mounted components on a compact PCB.
description_view_0: The front view shows the central IC chip, surface-mounted components, Intel branding, and gold contact fingers.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the module's thin profile and layered PCB construction.
description_view_2: The back view displays a flat PCB with a grid of solder points, Intel and Celeron branding, and no central chip.
description_view_3: The opposite side view again highlights the slimness and edge details of the module.
